lenses.guidance: vv 12-13's Levite-for-firstborn substitution — 'I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn' — establishes a redemption-by-substitute pattern. The shadow of substitutionary atonement runs from Passover lamb to Levite to Christ.

vv 1-4's recall of Aaron's sons — Nadab and Abihu having died — keeps the warning fresh. The priesthood roster echoes throughout Scripture as a vocational thread; 1 Chr 6 reproduces it; Heb 7 reads it as anticipating a different priest entirely.
